how_he_wins_notes: Elite frame and explosiveness show up immediately; he moves like a former safety, covers ground in a hurry, and can erase space from stack or overhang alignments when he sees it clean.
primary_concerns_notes: Coverage spacing and route-distribution discipline still have to stay clean because the role will ask him to process route stress, not just chase ball carriers.
role_projection_notes: Best as a space-match overhang backer who can live in big nickel, mug pressure looks, and still fold into true off-ball linebacker work on early downs.
all22_watch_points: Key/read speed; route carry from overhang alignments; strike timing vs blocks; pressure timing; finish in space
voice_notes: Use top-shelf athletic but controlled language; former safety movement matters; avoid generic chase-backer phrasing.

how_he_wins_notes: Hybrid LB/EDGE traits show up in the way he closes, bends, and jolts contact; range and knock-back power let him affect plays from depth or as a stand-up pressure piece.
primary_concerns_notes: Hybrid deployment can blur the projection if the rush plan and coverage feel do not stay balanced; he still has to prove the full menu versus NFL structure stress.
role_projection_notes: Stand-up SAM/EDGE hybrid in a multiple front with real green-dog, mugged-A-gap, and space-rush utility rather than a static stack linebacker projection.
all22_watch_points: Rush arc; contact power; knock-back through tight ends and backs; off-ball trigger speed; coverage feel when widened
voice_notes: Lean into hybrid pressure SAM/EDGE language; Micah-Parsons-style deployment is internal context only, not a public comp quote.

how_he_wins_notes: Complete back profile: sees the alley, creates hidden yards through contact, and brings enough pass-protection value to stay on the field in real passing situations.
primary_concerns_notes: The projection only stays premium if the pass game and protection value continue to show up consistently against NFL size and pressure disguise.
role_projection_notes: Feature back in a wide-zone / play-action system who can handle early-down creation and still stay on the field when the protection menu expands.
all22_watch_points: Vision at the mesh; pad level through contact; scan rules in pass pro; route detail out of the backfield; finish speed in the open field
voice_notes: Do not undersell his pass-pro value; complete-back language is warranted.

all22_watch_points: Release menu; leverage attack; break mechanics; contact-point finish; press answers
voice_notes: Keep language receiver-specific and route-craft focused

all22_watch_points: Deep range; trigger downhill; slot/fit versatility; ball tracking; tackle finish
voice_notes: Stress coverage versatility and trigger speed

all22_watch_points: Get-off; counter depth; finish through contact; early-down edge setting
voice_notes: Keep edge language tied to true pass sets

how_he_wins_notes: Explosion, violence, and power show up fast; he wins through the chest, compresses rush space, and plays through contact with the type of urgency that disrupts both run and pass downs.
primary_concerns_notes: Length/prototype questions will stay in the conversation, so he has to keep proving that burst, leverage, and play strength are enough to beat the measurables concerns.
role_projection_notes: Every-down edge who can reduce inside on passing downs and win with power-first rush sequencing rather than a pure speed-only profile.
all22_watch_points: First-step urgency; leverage through contact; rush counters after initial collision; edge-setting discipline; finish through the pocket
voice_notes: Treat as an exception-profile defender if the tape keeps validating the production.

how_he_wins_notes: He is quicker than fast and wins with savvy separation skill, body control, and route manipulation; the game looks easy for him because he gets in and out of cuts without losing rhythm.
primary_concerns_notes: Frame and play style put pressure on timing, route detail, and catch consistency because he is not built like a classic prototype outside winner.
role_projection_notes: Movement Z / slot mover who can stress leverage from reduced splits, motion, and route-volume usage rather than a pure size-speed isolation role.
all22_watch_points: Stem pacing; leverage wins; transition quickness; release detail; catch-point consistency through contact
voice_notes: Savvy separator, compact frame, quicker-than-fast language; avoid calling him a burner.

how_he_wins_notes: Versatile DB with real range and movement skill; can overlap from depth, play through the ball, and still hold up when the role asks him to rotate or drive from split structures.
primary_concerns_notes: Fast is not the same as fluid, so the projection still depends on transitions, angle discipline, and role fit staying as clean as the testing profile suggests.
role_projection_notes: Multipurpose safety with centerfield range and interchangeable usage, but the deployment should still protect him from being treated as a do-everything gadget comp by default.
all22_watch_points: Transition fluidity; route overlap from depth; tackling angles in space; disguise into rotation; ball production timing
voice_notes: Avoid lazy DeJean-style shorthand in public copy; keep it to role versatility and movement skill.

how_he_wins_notes: Catch-point talent and body control jump off the screen; he can win above the rim, finish outside his frame, and stress coverage with size, stride length, and vertical ball skills.
primary_concerns_notes: Maturity and week-to-week consistency remain part of the evaluation, so the projection depends on the day-to-day approach matching the raw talent.
role_projection_notes: Boundary vertical winner who can threaten downfield and at the catch point, with developmental upside tied to consistency and route detail.
all22_watch_points: Release urgency; stack ability; catch-point timing; route detail on in-breakers; snap-to-snap competitive consistency
voice_notes: Use catch-point language and consistency concerns; do not soften the maturity question if it is part of the file.

how_he_wins_notes: Rare size-athletic combination changes the geometry; he can create outside structure, stress pursuit with his legs, and generate explosive offense when the pocket breaks down.
primary_concerns_notes: The athlete is rare, but the passing profile is still raw; ball placement, timing, and down-to-down decision-making need to become much more stable.
role_projection_notes: Developmental movement-passer with designed-run value and off-schedule creation, but the projection still depends on real growth from structure.
all22_watch_points: Pocket patience; first-window trust; off-platform accuracy; turnover decisions under pressure; designed-run feel
voice_notes: Keep rare-athlete language, but anchor the concerns in actual passing consistency.

how_he_wins_notes: Mismatch athleticism is obvious; he can outrun second-level defenders, stress angles after the catch, and create chunk plays with burst that most tight ends do not have.
primary_concerns_notes: The athletic advantage only becomes premium if the hands stay more consistent and the every-down role does not outrun his current frame.
role_projection_notes: Detach/move tight end with real vertical stress and catch-and-run juice, with inline usage best deployed selectively rather than as a static Y-only role.
all22_watch_points: Hands at the catch point; route acceleration through the seam; play strength on inline contacts; YAC balance
voice_notes: Brock Bowers body-type neighborhood is useful internally, not as a public direct comp.

how_he_wins_notes: Produces pressure with first-step urgency, real cornering ability, and enough speed-to-power to keep tackles from sitting on one answer.
primary_concerns_notes: The projection depends on sustaining run-game discipline and stacking a full rush menu so the disruption translates snap after snap against NFL tackles.
role_projection_notes: Every-down edge with true pass-set value who can align wide, threaten up the field, and still play through contact well enough to stay out there on base downs.
all22_watch_points: Rush arc; counters after first step; run-fit edge integrity; finish through contact; tackle set manipulation
voice_notes: Keep the tone aggressive but technical; his pass-rush value should sound translatable, not hype-driven.
